nodes.x: But now they that are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to have set with the dogs of my flock.
Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.
And they were haughty, and committed abomination before me: therefore I took them away as I saw good.
Yet they hearkened not unto me, nor inclined their ear, but hardened their neck: they did worse than their fathers.

I soon discovered among the mothers, a kind of carelessness with
regard to their children, even when their lives were in danger. So
I called them together, and endeavored to impress upon their minds
the importance of doing their duty to their children; that in such a
place as this, especially, they ought to keep them constantly by their
side; that they should consider that children were given to…
Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth: yet they have not prevailed against me.
Yea, whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom old age was perished?

There are hundreds of Elders here who have traveled through England, Scotland, Wales, Germany, Switzerland, Norway and elsewhere, who know very well that the people were found in most of those lands in circumstances of slavery—bondage far worse than the “blacks” in the South previous to their liberation. There was nothing before the people but the prospect of starvation; and they were subject to…
Thus saith the Lord of hosts; There shall yet old men and old women dwell in the streets of Jerusalem, and every man with his staff in his hand for very age.

means to carry on this work; and that means I shall require, from this day forth, to be at my control, and not at the beck and call of every Elder; and at the same time they make me gather the poor and pay all the debts of the Church. We want to build the Temple walls on this block. We now support thousands of people, and wish to support thousands more. They want hats, coats, shirts, dresses,…

Elders Riser and Secrist have laboured faithfully, through much privation, persecution, bonds, and Imprisonment, for the Gospel's sake and the salvation of immortal souls in those countries. Their labours, though much like seed cast into the ground, will yet bring forth thirty, sixty, and an hundred fold to the glory of God, and their eternal rejoicing. They go with our confidence and blessing,…
For want and famine they were solitary; fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste.
Therefore, they tarried in the wilderness, or did not travel a direct course, and were afflicted with hunger and thirst, because of their transgressions.

Therefore, as they were unfaithful they did not prosper nor progress in their journey, but were driven back, and incurred the displeasure of God upon them; and therefore they were smitten with famine and sore afflictions, to stir them up in remembrance of their duty.
Wherefore they arose and fled in the twilight, and left their tents, and their horses, and their asses, even the camp as it was, and fled for their life.
And it came to pass that the wild game became scarce in the wilderness insomuch that the robbers were about to perish with hunger.
Who cut up mallows by the bushes, and juniper roots for their meat.
And many spread their garments in the way: and others cut down branches off the trees, and strawed them in the way.
For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither as the green herb.
Why hast thou then broken down her hedges, so that all they which pass by the way do pluck her?
And the enemy came by night, and broke down the hedge; and the servants of the nobleman arose and were affrighted, and fled; and the enemy destroyed their works, and broke down the olive trees.
They were driven forth from among men, (they cried after them as after a thief;)
And they went out quickly, and fled from the sepulchre; for they trembled and were amazed: neither said they any thing to any man; for they were afraid.
Then they cried unto the Lord in their trouble, and he saved them out of their distresses.
Then they cried unto the Lord in their trouble, and he delivered them out of their distresses.

After the mob had ceased yelling, and retired ; and while evening was spreading her dark mantle over the unblushing scenery, as if to bide it from the gaze of day; men, women and children, who had been driven or frightened from their homes, by yells and threats, began to return from their hiding place*, in thickets, corn-fields, woods and groves, and view with heavy hearts the scenery of…
To dwell in the clifts of the valleys, in caves of the earth, and in the rocks.
To go into the clefts of the rocks, and into the tops of the ragged rocks, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of his majesty, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.

And they shall go into the holes of the rocks, and into the caves of the earth, for the fear of the Lord shall come upon them and the glory of his majesty shall smite them, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.

To go into the clefts of the rocks, and into the tops of the ragged rocks, for the fear of the Lord shall come upon them and the majesty of his glory shall smite them, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.
And they shall go into the holes of the rocks, and into the caves of the earth, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of his majesty, when he ariseth to shake terribly the earth.

_The hollow way at Kuessnacht. You descend from behind amid rocks; and
travellers, before appearing on the scene, are seen from the height
above. Rocks encircle the whole space; on one of the foremost is a
projecting crag overgrown with brushwood._
Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.
And they gathered them together upon heaps: and the land stank.
And they that have been scattered shall be gathered.
And they gathered themselves together again, and put on their armor, and went forth against the Lamanites to drive them out of their land.

‘them up green he commanded to stand apart, and 
those who had given them up dry and cut he 
sommanded to stand with the first ones. 6. Then
They were children of fools, yea, children of base men: they were viler than the earth.
And they have brought forth children; yea, even the family of all the earth.
And when these men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them, the sons of men saw that those daughters were fair, and they took them wives, even as they chose.
And thus he commanded them to preach. And thus they became the children of God.
And now am I their song, yea, I am their byword.
Behold their sitting down, and their rising up; I am their musick.
I was a derision to all my people; and their song all the day.
And it came to pass that when I had said these words, behold, the bands were loosed from off my hands and feet, and I stood before my brethren, and I spake unto them again.
They are of the world: therefore speak they of the world, and the world heareth them.
They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face.
They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.
And they have not cried unto me with their heart, when they howled upon their beds: they assemble themselves for corn and wine, and they rebel against me.
Woe unto them! for they have fled from me: destruction unto them! because they have transgressed against me: though I have redeemed them, yet they have spoken lies against me.

And yet for all that, when they be in the land of their enemies, I will not cast them away, neither will I abhor them, to destroy them utterly, and to break my covenant with them: for I am the Lord their God.
Every day they wrest my words: all their thoughts are against me for evil.
Because he hath loosed my cord, and afflicted me, they have also let loose the bridle before me.
Because he slew me not from the womb; or that my mother might have been my grave, and her womb to be always great with me.
Because he hath inclined his ear unto me, therefore will I call upon him as long as I live.
I poured out my complaint before him; I shewed before him my trouble.
Also they reported his good deeds before me, and uttered my words to him. And Tobiah sent letters to put me in fear.
Upon my right hand rise the youth; they push away my feet, and they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.
¶ And, behold, an hand touched me, which set me upon my knees and upon the palms of my hands.
Their hand shall be lifted up upon their adversaries, and all their enemies shall be cut off.
I have spread out my hands all the day unto a rebellious people, which walketh in a way that was not good, after their own thoughts;
They mar my path, they set forward my calamity, they have no helper.
They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the Lord was my stay.
They run and prepare themselves without my fault: awake to help me, and behold.
Lord, they have killed thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am left alone, and they seek my life.
And it came to pass that I called after them, and they did hear me; wherefore they did cease to flee from my presence.
They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of mine head: they that would destroy me, being mine enemies wrongfully, are mighty: then I restored that which I took not away.
They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.
They came round about me daily like water; they compassed me about together.

And now it came to pass that when I had spoken these words they were angry with me, and were desirous to throw me into the depths of the sea; and as they came forth to lay their hands upon me I spake unto them, saying: In the name of the Almighty God, I command you that ye touch me not, for I am filled with the power of God, even unto the consuming of my flesh; and whoso shall lay his hands upon…

Wherefore, they knew not whither they should steer the ship, insomuch that there arose a great storm, yea, a great and terrible tempest, and we were driven back upon the waters for the space of three days; and they began to be frightened exceedingly lest they should be drowned in the sea; nevertheless they did not loose me.
Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul.
The waters compassed me about, even to the soul: the depth closed me round about, the weeds were wrapped about my head.
Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.
Be not a terror unto me: thou art my hope in the day of evil.
Thy fierce wrath goeth over me; thy terrors have cut me off.
Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall drive him to his feet.
Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.
And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.
And it came to pass that I was overcome because of my afflictions, for I considered that mine afflictions were great above all, because of the destruction of my people, for I had beheld their fall.
Before I was afflicted I went astray: but now have I kept thy word.
And now, behold, the promise of the Lord is fulfilled, and ye are smitten and afflicted.
My bones are pierced in me in the night season: and my sinews take no rest.
For my days are consumed like smoke, and my bones are burned as an hearth.
My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.
There is no soundness in my flesh because of thine anger; neither is there any rest in my bones because of my sin.
And when I beheld, lo, the sinews and the flesh came up upon them, and the skin covered them above: but there was no breath in them.
I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart is like wax; it is melted in the midst of my bowels.
By the great force of my disease is my garment changed: it bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.
And the garment, either warp, or woof, or whatsoever thing of skin it be, which thou shalt wash, if the plague be departed from them, then it shall be washed the second time, and shall be clean.
I made sackcloth also my garment; and I became a proverb to them.
¶ The garment also that the plague of leprosy is in, whether it be a woollen garment, or a linen garment;
And if the priest shall look, and, behold, the plague be not spread in the garment, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in any thing of skin;
He hath cast me into the mire, and I am become like dust and ashes.
Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as the clay; and wilt thou bring me into dust again?
And he hath made my mouth like a sharp sword; in the shadow of his hand hath he hid me, and made me a polished shaft; in his quiver hath he hid me;
Sharp stones are under him: he spreadeth sharp pointed things upon the mire.
And Abraham answered and said, Behold now, I have taken upon me to speak unto the Lord, which am but dust and ashes:
Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare raiment as the clay;
I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.
Unto thee will I cry, O Lord my rock; be not silent to me: lest, if thou be silent to me, I become like them that go down into the pit.
Lord, I cry unto thee: make haste unto me; give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.
Therefore pray not thou for this people, neither lift up cry nor prayer for them, neither make intercession to me: for I will not hear thee.
Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.
For I said in my haste, I am cut off from before thine eyes: nevertheless thou heardest the voice of my supplications when I cried unto thee.
Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me.
And if thou deal thus with me, kill me, I pray thee, out of hand, if I have found favour in thy sight; and let me not see my wretchedness.
Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned thyself from the fierceness of thine anger.
I say, sayest thou, (but they are but vain words) I have counsel and strength for war: now on whom dost thou trust, that thou rebellest against me?
¶ O Lord, thou hast deceived me, and I was deceived: thou art stronger than I, and hast prevailed: I am in derision daily, every one mocketh me.
Thou hast a mighty arm: strong is thy hand, and high is thy right hand.
Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to ride upon it, and dissolvest my substance.
And that bringeth me forth from mine enemies: thou also hast lifted me up on high above them that rose up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that abundance of waters may cover thee?
I will extol thee, O Lord; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.
Unstable as water, thou shalt not excel; because thou wentest up to thy father’s bed; then defiledst thou it: he went up to my couch.
Unto thee lift I up mine eyes, O thou that dwellest in the heavens.
For I know that thou wilt bring me to death, and to the house appointed for all living.
And if thou art faithful unto the end thou shalt have a crown of immortality, and eternal life in the mansions which I have prepared in the house of my Father.
Who shall tell thee words, whereby thou and all thy house shall be saved.
For behold, I have accepted this house, and my name shall be here; and I will manifest myself to my people in mercy in this house.
Where thou diest, will I die, and there will I be buried: the Lord do so to me, and more also, if ought but death part thee and me.
Thou shalt be over my house, and according unto thy word shall all my people be ruled: only in the throne will I be greater than thou.
Howbeit he will not stretch out his hand to the grave, though they cry in his destruction.
Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.
And they shall scourge him, and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.
Yet shall he be brought to the grave, and shall remain in the tomb.
And all his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him; but he refused to be comforted; and he said, For I will go down into the grave unto my son mourning. Thus his father wept for him.
With a strong hand, and with a stretched out arm: for his mercy endureth for ever.
Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? was not my soul grieved for the poor?
And my soul was rent with anguish, because of the slain of my people, and I cried:
but could render him no assistance. My soul was grieved, I rose from my bed, and spent the remainder of the night in walking the floor.
This poor man cried, and the Lord heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.
In my distress I cried unto the Lord, and he heard me.
Because I delivered the poor that cried, and the fatherless, and him that had none to help him.
When I looked for good, then evil came unto me: and when I waited for light, there came darkness.
And I, God, saw the light; and that light was good. And I, God, divided the light from the darkness.
I find then a law, that, when I would do good, evil is present with me.
And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.
But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If, therefore, the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that darkness!
But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that darkness!
My bowels boiled, and rested not: the days of affliction prevented me.
For all the day long have I been plagued, and chastened every morning.
Remembering mine affliction and my misery, the wormwood and the gall.
I went mourning without the sun: I stood up, and I cried in the congregation.
And all the congregation lifted up their voice, and cried; and the people wept that night.

And the earth did cleave together again, that it stood; and the mourning, and the weeping, and the wailing of the people who were spared alive did cease; and their mourning was turned into joy, and their lamentations into the praise and thanksgiving unto the Lord Jesus Christ, their Redeemer.
And said, I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the Lord, and he heard me; out of the belly of hell cried I, and thou heardest my voice.

And I cried with a loud voice,
    with the spirit of power,
    And blessed and glorified and extolled.
I am a brother to dragons, and a companion to owls.
The little owl, and the great owl, and the swan,
The beast of the field shall honour me, the dragons and the owls: because I give waters in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, my chosen.
And the little owl, and the cormorant, and the great owl,
Praise the Lord from the earth, ye dragons, and all deeps:
My skin is black upon me, and my bones are burned with heat.
Our skin was black like an oven because of the terrible famine.
¶ Or if there be any flesh, in the skin whereof there is a hot burning, and the quick flesh that burneth have a white bright spot, somewhat reddish, or white;
My harp also is turned to mourning, and my organ into the voice of them that weep.

And I will turn your feasts into mourning, and all your songs into lamentation; and I will bring up sackcloth upon all loins, and baldness upon every head; and I will make it as the mourning of an only son, and the end thereof as a bitter day.
And I will cause the noise of thy songs to cease; and the sound of thy harps shall be no more heard.
I am troubled; I am bowed down greatly; I go mourning all the day long.
They take the timbrel and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the organ.
